evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Ensure data quality, consistency, and availability
evidence.domain_risk: Design and maintain pipelines between core systems (POS, e-commerce, ERP, OMS, CRM, BI)
evidence.data_team_maturity: Develop data lake and BI environments (Fivetran, Databricks, dbt)
evidence.jd_authorship: Build integrations using APIs and Celigo middleware; Develop data lake and BI environments (Fivetran, Databricks, dbt)
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Develop data lake and BI environments (Fivetran, Databricks, dbt)
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Build integrations using APIs; Enhance performance and maintainability
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Building new API integrations is combined with enhancing the performance and maintainability of existing systems, reflecting both greenfield and fix/scale work.
evidence.ai_role: Develop data lake and BI environments (Fivetran, Databricks, dbt)
evidence.ai_role_explanation: Standard data platform tooling with no AI-first mindset, AI tool usage, or GenAI infrastructure signals.
evidence.testing_framing: Ensure data quality, consistency, and availability
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Ownership verb 'Ensure' paired with quality indicates direct responsibility for quality practice.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Ensure data quality, consistency, and availability; Design and maintain pipelines between core systems
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Operational reliability framing (pipeline stability, data quality) dominates; no regulatory or compliance emphasis.
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Multiple rigour signals (data quality, consistency, robustness, standards, monitoring) with no velocity language present.
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: E-commerce/retail operations impact business decisions and inventory management but contain no financial reporting, regulatory, or compliance language.
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Named tools in responsibilities (Fivetran, Databricks, dbt, Celigo) indicate established infrastructure being scaled, not greenfield build.
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Specific technical details (named systems, named tools, middleware preference) allow clear understanding of day-to-day work despite some generic collaboration bullets.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The primary mission is building data infrastructure (data lake, pipelines, BI environments) for consumption by internal data consumers and analytics teams.
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: The role combines strategic ownership via infrastructure development and standard-setting with execution of business requirements translated from Product Owners.
